South Indian Bank Junior Officer (Operations) Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

The South Indian Bank Limited, a Premier Scheduled Commercial Bank in India, has officially released a recruitment notification for the post of Junior Officer (Operations) for the Hyderabad and Vijayawada Regions covering the states of Andhra Pradesh and Telangana. The notification includes complete details on eligibility, terms of employment, salary/compensation, mode of selection, application fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification carefully before applying online. The notification is available on the official South Indian Bank recruitment website.

  • Download South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

South Indian Bank Junior Officer (Operations) Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organization Name The South Indian Bank Ltd.
Post Name Junior Officer (Operations)
Recruitment Region Hyderabad and Vijayawada Regions (States of Andhra Pradesh & Telangana)
Type of Employment Contract - initially for 3 years, renewable at Bank's sole discretion based on performance
Career Progression High performing candidates may be considered for regular employment as Assistant Manager (Scale I) after contract period
Pay / CTC Rs. 4.86 lakhs to Rs. 5.04 lakhs per annum (inclusive of NPS contribution, Insurance Premium and Variable Pay)
Application Mode Online only (via South Indian Bank official recruitment portal)
Selection Mode Online Test and/or Group Discussion + Preliminary and/or Final Interview
Application Fee NIL
Official Website https://recruit.southindianbank.bank.in/RDC/

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Online Application - Start Date 06-05-2026
Online Application - End Date 13-05-2026
Eligibility Criteria Calculated As On 31-03-2026
Online Test / Group Discussion Date To be notified on Bank's official website
Interview Date To be notified on Bank's official website

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on 31.03.2026. Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply. Candidates who have previously attended Group Discussion / Personal Interview for the Junior Officers role as per any previous notification of the Bank and were not included in the final selection list are not eligible to apply for this notification. Proficiency in local language (Telugu) will be given preference. All eligibility is provisional and subject to verification of original documents when a candidate reports for interview/training/joining.

Educational Qualification

Qualification Details
Minimum Qualification Any Graduation with minimum 50% marks from a recognized university.
Stream of Education Regular stream only: SSLC/SSC, HSC and Graduation (10+2+ Graduation under regular stream).
Work Experience Minimum 1 year of experience in Bank / NBFCs.
Special Note Candidates who have attended Group Discussion / Personal Interview for Junior Officers role as per previous notification(s) and were not included in the final selection list are NOT eligible to apply.

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

The maximum age limit is 28 years as on 31.03.2026. Age relaxation of 5 years is available for SC/ST candidates. Candidates belonging to SC/ST must submit attested copies of certificates from a competent authority to prove their status as and when called for.

Category Age Limit
General (UR) Maximum 28 years (as on 31.03.2026)
SC / ST 5 years of age relaxation (maximum 33 years)

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

No application fee is required for this post. Candidates can apply free of cost through the official South Indian Bank recruitment portal.

Category Recruitment Fee
All Categories NIL (No Application Fee)

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 - Terms of Employment

Selected candidates will be appointed on a contract basis initially for a period of 3 years, renewable at the sole discretion of the Bank based on performance evaluation. On completion of the initial contract period or any extension, high performing candidates may, without any obligation and in the Bank's sole and absolute discretion, be considered for regular employment as Assistant Manager (Scale I), subject to the then prevailing norms of the Bank. There will be no mandatory minimum service period as stipulated in the case of staff members recruited under regular employment.

Parameter Details
Designation / Job Role Junior Officer (Operations)
Term of Appointment Contract - initially 3 years, renewable at Bank's sole discretion based on performance
Career Progression High performers may be considered for regular employment as Assistant Manager (Scale I) after contract period, at Bank's sole discretion
Posting Location Anywhere in Andhra Pradesh & Telangana as per Bank's requirement; liable to be transferred anywhere in India at Bank's sole discretion
Total CTC Rs. 4.86 lakhs to Rs. 5.04 lakhs per annum (inclusive of NPS contribution, Insurance Premium and Variable Pay based on performance)
Official Duty Allowance Eligible Lodging, Halting and Travelling allowance for official travel, as per Bank norms
Insurance Group Medical Insurance and Group Accident Insurance at the cost of the Bank; Group Life Insurance with premium borne by the employee
Increment Annual increment during contract period based on performance evaluation or other parameters as decided by the Bank

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 consists of an Online Test and/or Group Discussion, followed by a Preliminary and/or Final Interview. The Online Test and Group Discussion are elimination stages - only test-qualified candidates will be called for Group Discussion or Personal Interview, and only candidates shortlisted from the Group Discussion will be advanced to the Personal Interview. The Bank reserves the right to modify the selection process based on the number of applications received. Mere eligibility will not vest any right on the applicant to be called for the Recruitment Process. All marks/scores/grades at any stage shall be treated as strictly confidential.

Stage Details Nature
Stage 1 Online Test (if conducted) Elimination Stage - conducted at locations identified by the Bank
Stage 2 Group Discussion (if conducted) Elimination Stage - only Online Test qualified candidates called
Stage 3 Preliminary and/or Final Personal Interview Only Group Discussion shortlisted candidates advanced

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 Online Application Form

Eligible candidates who wish to apply for the South Indian Bank Junior Officer (Operations) posts can do so by submitting their application form online. The application process is online only through the official South Indian Bank recruitment portal, and candidates must ensure that they complete all steps before the closing date.

The application form for the South Indian Bank Junior Officer (Operations) Recruitment 2026 is available on the official South Indian Bank recruitment website at https://recruit.southindianbank.bank.in/RDC/. Eligible candidates with any Graduation (minimum 50% marks) and minimum 1 year of experience in Bank/NBFCs are encouraged to fill out the online application form and upload the required documents before the last date.

Online Apply Link for South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026

How to Apply for South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026

Applicants can apply online only through the Bank's official recruitment website at https://recruit.southindianbank.bank.in/RDC/ from 06-05-2026 to 13-05-2026. No other mode of application will be accepted. Before registering, applicants must ensure they have a valid Email ID and mobile number in their name. An applicant can only apply for vacancies in one State - making multiple registrations will result in disqualification.

There is no provision to modify the submitted online application - candidates are requested to fill in the application form with utmost care. After successful submission, a system generated User ID (Application Ref. ID) will be generated - candidates must note this for future reference. An email with the application PDF and login credentials will be sent to the registered email ID upon successful submission. The physical copy of the application need not be sent to the Bank.

Documents / Upload Specifications:

  • Photograph: Recent passport size colour photo, white background, no caps/hats/dark glasses - Resolution: 378 x 437 pixels, maximum 50 KB (JPEG/JPG format)
  • Signature: Signed on white paper with black ink pen - Resolution: 140 x 110 pixels, maximum 50 KB
  • Resume: PDF format, file size not exceeding 1 MB
  • Education Documents: Certificates and marklists of X/SSLC (must include D.O.B), XII/HSC and final marklist and certificate of Graduation - combined as a single PDF file, not exceeding 3 MB

Documents to be Produced When Called for (Originals):

  • Proof of age (SSLC/SSC certificate with Date of Birth)
  • Educational qualification certificates and marklists (X, XII, Graduation)
  • Work experience certificate(s) proving minimum 1 year in Bank/NBFCs
  • SC/ST certificate from competent authority (if applicable)
  • Proof of nationality / domicile as applicable
  • Any other documents as required by the Bank

For queries, contact: Toll Free: 1800-425-1809 / 1800-102-9408, 0484-277-1383 | Email: [email protected]

Apply Online at: https://recruit.southindianbank.bank.in/RDC/

Important Instructions for South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Beware of fraudulent job alerts - apply only through the official online portal. Applications through any other mode will not be accepted.
  • Candidates who have previously attended Group Discussion / Personal Interview for Junior Officers role as per any previous notification and were not included in the final selection list are NOT eligible to apply.
  • An applicant can apply for vacancies in only one State - multiple registrations will lead to disqualification.
  • There is no provision to modify the submitted online application - fill in all details carefully before submission.
  • No application fee is required for this post.
  • Appointment is subject to Medical fitness, satisfactory background verification including CIBIL/credit history check, police clearance, and completion of other formalities as per Bank rules.
  • Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification.
  • Applicants must appear for the Recruitment Process at their own cost.
  • Call letters/advice will be sent by email only - no hard copy will be sent. Candidates are advised to check their registered email and the Bank's official website regularly.
  • All marks/scores/grades obtained at any stage of the selection process are strictly confidential and will not be disclosed to any party including candidates.
  • Applicants are advised to avoid last-minute rush and submit the online application well before the closing date, as website access may be delayed due to heavy internet traffic near the deadline.
  • The Bank reserves the right to make modifications to the selection process and the qualifying criteria at any stage, and such decisions will be final and binding.

How to Apply for Sarkari Job Online 2026

  1. Find the right Sarkari Job: Use the vacancy table or sector links above on OnlyJobs. Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, or Engineering. Check the last date and eligibility carefully.

  2. Read the official notification: Click on the job listing to open the official notification PDF. Read all eligibility conditions — age limit, educational qualification, experience, category relaxations.

  3. Visit the official recruitment website: Never apply through third-party sites. Use the direct official link provided on OnlyJobs. Look for "Apply Online" or "Register" on the official portal.

  4. Register / Create account: Sign up using your valid email ID and active mobile number. Keep your Aadhaar, bank account details, and email accessible.

  5. Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualifications, category (General/OBC/SC/ST/EWS), address, and other required information accurately.

  6. Upload documents: Scan and upload recent passport-size photograph, signature, 10th certificate (for DOB proof), educational certificates, caste certificate (if applicable). Check file size and format (JPG/PDF as specified).

  7. Pay application fee: Pay via Net Banking, UPI, Debit/Credit Card as applicable. Some categories (SC/ST/PwD/Women) may be exempted from fee. Keep the payment receipt.

  8. Submit and save confirmation: Review all details before final submission. After submitting, download the application printout / PDF confirmation with your registration number. Store it safely for admit card, exam, and future reference.

Why Sarkari Job? Benefits of Government Employment in India

Job Security
Permanent employment — cannot be terminated without due process. Lifelong career stability.
7th Pay Commission Salary
Fixed salary + DA, HRA, TA allowances. DA revised every 6 months. Regular pay revisions.
Medical Benefits
Free or subsidised medical treatment for employee and family under CGHS / state health schemes.
Housing Benefits
Government quarters (where available) or HRA (8–27% of basic pay). No private rent worries.
Pension (NPS / OPS)
Post-retirement monthly pension under NPS or OPS (state-specific). Financial security after service.
Work-Life Balance
Fixed working hours. Paid casual, medical, earned leave. National and state holidays. No overtime culture.
Career Growth
Regular promotions through DPC, ACR-based upgrades, MACP benefits (3 financial upgrades guaranteed).
Children's Education Allowance
Reimbursement of children's school fees (₹2,812/month per child up to 2 children, revised periodically).

Frequently Asked Questions – Sarkari Job 2026

What is Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी)?

Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी) refers to government employment in India — jobs offered by central government departments, state governments, public sector undertakings (PSUs), banks, railways, defence forces, and other government-funded organisations. These jobs offer permanent employment, fixed salary as per 7th Pay Commission, pension, medical benefits, and unmatched job security.

How to find Sarkari Job vacancy 2026 online?

To find online Sarkari Job vacancies in 2026: (1) Visit OnlyJobs daily for updated notifications. (2) Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, PG. (3) Filter by sector — Railway, Banking, SSC, Defence, State PSC, Teaching. (4) Check last dates carefully. (5) Apply through the official department website using the direct link provided. Enable push notifications on OnlyJobs for instant alerts.

What Sarkari Job can I get after 10th pass?

After 10th pass, you can apply for: Railway Group D (RRB), Indian Army Soldier posts, Postal Mail Guard, SSC MTS / Havaldar, Constable GD, Anganwadi Worker/Helper, ITI Apprentice in PSUs (IOCL, HPCL, NTPC), MCL / Coal India posts, and various state government peon/chowkidar posts. Thousands of 10th pass Sarkari Job vacancies open every year.

What is the salary in Sarkari Job 2026?

Sarkari Job salary in 2026 follows the 7th Pay Commission: Group D / MTS: ₹22,000–₹26,000/month. Clerk / Group C: ₹32,000–₹48,000/month. Officer Grade (SSC CGL, Bank PO): ₹47,000–₹85,000/month. IAS/IPS (UPSC): ₹56,100–₹1,44,200 basic pay + DA, HRA, TA, medical and other allowances. State government salaries vary by state pay commission.

Which Bharat Sarkari Job has the most vacancies in 2026?

The largest Bharat Sarkari Job recruitments in 2026 include: Indian Army Agniveer (40,000+ posts), RRB Group D (22,000+ posts), SSC CGL (14,582 posts), SSC MTS (10,000+ posts), UP Police Constable (multiple), Bihar Police (multiple), IBPS Clerk (8,000+ posts), and various state PSC recruitments. Total active sarkari vacancies in India exceed 52,920 as of March 2026.

What is the best website to find Sarkari Job in India?

OnlyJobs is India's most trusted portal for Sarkari Job notifications since 2011, serving over 10 million users. Official direct sources include: ssc.nic.in (SSC), indianrailways.gov.in (Railways), upsc.gov.in (UPSC), ibps.in (Banking), and respective state PSC websites. OnlyJobs aggregates all official notifications in one place with direct apply links.

How to apply for Sarkari Job online in 2026?

To apply for Sarkari Job online: (1) Read official notification PDF. (2) Check eligibility. (3) Visit official recruitment website. (4) Register with email/mobile. (5) Fill application form. (6) Upload photo, signature, and certificates. (7) Pay fee via Net Banking/UPI/Debit card. (8) Submit and lication confirmation. Save the registration number for admit card and future reference.

What are the benefits of Sarkari Job in India?

Key benefits: (1) Lifelong job security. (2) 7th Pay Commission salary + DA revised biannually. (3) HRA (8–27% of basic). (4) Medical benefits (CGHS). (5) Pension under NPS/OPS. (6) Paid leave — casual, earned, medical. (7) Gratuity after 5 years. (8) Children's education allowance. (9) Fixed working hours and work-life balance. (10) Prestige and social recognition in Indian society.

What is the age limit for Sarkari Job?

Age limits vary by post: Group D (18–25 years), SSC CGL (18–32 years), Bank PO (20–30 years), UPSC Civil Services (21–32 years), Defence Agniveer (17.5–23 years). Age relaxations: SC/ST — 5 years, OBC — 3 years, PwD — 10 years, Ex-Servicemen — 3 years (general), Government servants — up to 5 years. Always verify in the official notification for each post.

What is Bharat Sarkari Job?

Bharat Sarkari Job means government job opportunities across the entire country of India (Bharat) — spanning central and state government vacancies across all 28 states and 8 union territories. The term emphasises pan-India coverage. OnlyJobs covers all Bharat Sarkari Job notifications from UPSC, SSC, Railways, Banking, Defence, state PSCs, and all other government departments.

About OnlyJobs 2026

OnlyJobs (Government Jobs) are highly sought-after employment opportunities in India. These positions offer job security, good salary packages, allowances, and retirement benefits. Our platform provides comprehensive information about all types of government vacancies including:

  • Central Government Jobs: UPSC, SSC, Railway, Banking, Defence, PSUs

Frequently Asked Questions - OnlyJobs 2026

Q1: What is the eligibility for OnlyJobs?

Eligibility varies by position. Generally, candidates need to be Indian citizens aged 18-35 years (with age relaxation for reserved categories). Educational qualifications range from 10th pass to post-graduation depending on the job.

Q2: How to prepare for Government Job exams?

Preparation includes understanding the exam pattern, studying from standard books, practicing previous year papers, taking mock tests, and staying updated with current affairs. Focus on subjects like General Knowledge, Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, and English.

Q3: Which are the best Government Jobs in 2026?

Top government jobs include IAS/IPS (UPSC), Bank PO, SSC CGL, Railway Group A & B, Defence Services, PSU Jobs, State PCS, and Teaching positions. These offer excellent career growth, job security, and benefits.

Q4: What is the application process for OnlyJobs?

The process typically involves: 1) Online registration on the official website, 2) Filling application form, 3) Uploading documents (photo, signature, certificates), 4) Payment of application fee, 5) Downloading admit card, 6) Appearing for examination, 7) Document verification if selected.

Q5: How often are new OnlyJobs notifications released?

New government job notifications are released daily. Major recruitments like UPSC, SSC, IBPS have annual calendars, while state governments and PSUs release notifications throughout the year. We update our portal daily with 696+ active job listings.

WCD Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Apply Online wcd.nic.in

WCD Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 – Apply online for the latest Anganwadi Worker, Anganwadi Helper and Supervisor vacancies through the official WCD/ICDS portals such as wcd.nic.in and state ICDS portals. Candidates can check state-wise and district-wise Anganwadi vacancy lists, eligibility, qualifications, age limit, salary, and online application dates, and download the Anganwadi Application Form 2026. Start your career in Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) and stay updated with the latest Anganwadi job notifications from all major states including Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Telangana, Uttar Pradesh, Andhra Pradesh, Maharashtra, Bihar and more.

Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Overview

Anganwadi Worker Salary / Honorarium 2026

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers receive a monthly honorarium (not a salary), which includes a Central Government component and an additional State Government component. The table below shows the total honorarium per month for key states in 2026.

State Anganwadi Worker Anganwadi Helper Supervisor

Note: Honorarium figures are approximate and may vary. Refer to official state notification for exact figures. Additional benefits include ESI, provident fund (where applicable), and national health insurance.

Selection Process for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026

There is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Helper posts. Selection is merit-based on academic qualifications. For Supervisor and CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted.

How to Apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Online

  • Visit the official portal: Go to your state's official WCD / ICDS website or the direct link provided in the vacancy table above. For central information, visit wcd.nic.in.
  • Find the recruitment notification: Click on the "Recruitment" or "Anganwadi Recruitment 2026" section.
  • Download and read the notification: Download and read the official notification PDF carefully before applying.
  • Register / Login: Create a new account using your mobile number and Aadhaar. If already registered, log in with your credentials.
  • Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualification, address, and select your preferred district/ward. Ensure you apply only for a ward where you are a permanent resident.
  • Upload documents: Upload scanned copies of your passport-size photograph, signature, 10th/12th marksheet, Aadhaar card, and any caste/domicile certificate if required.
  • Review and submit: Check all entries carefully before final submission. Download and print the application confirmation / acknowledgement slip for your records.

About this page: Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 information is compiled by the OnlyJobs editorial team directly from official state WCD / ICDS recruitment notifications. We verify all vacancy details, eligibility, and last dates before publishing. Page is updated daily as new notifications are released. For any discrepancy, always refer to the official notification PDF linked in the apply column above.

Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 – FAQ's

How to apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 online?

Visit your state's official WCD / ICDS portal (link provided in table above). Register with mobile & Aadhaar, fill the application form, upload documents (photo, 10th certificate, Aadhaar, domicile), and submit before the last date. Print the application acknowledgement slip after submission.

What is the eligibility for Anganwadi Worker 2026?

Candidates must have passed 10th or 12th standard from a recognized board. Age limit is 18–35 years (varies by state). Must be a permanent resident of the ward/village for which vacancy exists. Only female candidates are eligible for Worker and Helper posts.

What is the salary of Anganwadi Worker in 2026?

Anganwadi Worker receives a monthly honorarium of ₹10,000–₹20,000 depending on the state (Karnataka and TN are highest at ~₹14,000). Anganwadi Helper gets ₹5,000–₹7,000/month. The Central Government pays ₹4,500/month as base honorarium, and states add their own component on top.

Is there a written exam for Anganwadi jobs?

No, there is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Anganwadi Helper posts. Selection is purely merit-based on academic qualification marks (10th / 12th percentage). For Supervisor / CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted depending on the state.

What is the age limit for Anganwadi Helper?

Age limit is 18 to 35 years as of 1st January 2026 in most states. SC/ST candidates get 5 years relaxation (maximum age 40). OBC candidates get 3 years relaxation (maximum age 38). PwD candidates get additional relaxation as per state rules.

What documents are required for Anganwadi job application?

Required documents: Aadhaar card, 10th/12th marksheet and certificate, domicile/residence certificate, caste certificate (SC/ST/OBC), passport-size photograph, scanned signature, and EWS/PwD certificate if applicable. Graduation certificate is needed for Supervisor post applications.

What is ICDS recruitment 2026?

ICDS stands for Integrated Child Development Services - the government programme under which Anganwadi centres operate nationwide. ICDS recruitment 2026 refers to hiring of Anganwadi Workers, Helpers, Supervisors, and CDPOs by state WCD departments under the Ministry of Women & Child Development (wcd.nic.in).

Is Anganwadi Worker a permanent government job?

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers are honorary workers, not regular government employees. They receive a fixed monthly honorarium (not salary) and are entitled to benefits like ESI, health insurance, and maternity benefits. Supervisors and CDPOs are regular government employees in most states and receive full pay & grade pay.

Can I apply for Anganwadi job from a different district?

No. Anganwadi Workers and Helpers must be permanent residents of the same ward/village/district where the vacancy exists. A domicile or residence certificate is mandatory. Applications from outside the specified locality are rejected. For Supervisor/CDPO posts, the same district residency rule generally applies.

Forest Department Posts List

Forest departments work with a clear hierarchy from senior Indian Forest Service officers to field-level staff like Forest Guard and Forest Watcher. Understanding these posts helps candidates know growth opportunities and career progression in forest services.

  • Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (PCCF) / Head of Forest Force
  • Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (APCCF) / Additional Chief Conservator of Forests
  • Chief Conservator of Forests (CCF)
  • Conservator of Forests (CF)
  • Deputy Conservator of Forests (DCF) / Divisional Forest Officer (DFO)
  • Assistant Conservator of Forests (ACF)
  • Forest Range Officer (FRO) / Range Forest Officer (RFO)
  • Deputy Forest Ranger / Forest Inspector
  • Section Forest Officer / Forester / Van Daroga
  • Beat Forest Officer / Beat Guard / Forester Grade I
  • Forest Guard / Van Rakshak
  • Forest Watcher / Forest Worker / Plantation Worker
  • Forest Ranger (in states where used separately)
  • Field Guard

Most forest department recruitment notifications on this page focus on Forest Guard, Forester, Forest Range Officer, Field Guard and similar Group B, C and D posts.

Eligibility for Forest Department Jobs

Eligibility for forest department jobs depends on the post, state and recruitment authority. Below is a general idea of qualification and age for popular posts like Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer. Candidates should always check the official notification for detailed eligibility criteria.

Education Qualification for Forest Guard, Forester, Ranger

  • Forest Guard / Field Guard / Forest Watcher / Beat Guard: Usually 10th pass or 12th pass from a recognized board, knowledge of local language, and basic physical standards as per state rules.
  • Forester / Section Forest Officer / Deputy Forest Ranger: Generally 12th pass or graduate in any stream or science-related subjects, with physical standards and sometimes driving or technical skills depending on the post.
  • Forest Range Officer (RFO) / Forest Ranger / ACF: Normally graduate degree in Science, Agriculture, Forestry, Environmental Science, Engineering or related disciplines, with higher physical standards and sometimes a competitive exam similar to state PCS level.
  • IFS / Indian Forest Service and senior posts (DCF, CF, CCF, PCCF): Filled through UPSC Indian Forest Service Examination and promotions, requiring a graduate degree and all-India competitive selection.

Age Limit & Relaxation

  • Forest Guard / Forester / Field Guard: Minimum age is usually 18 years and maximum age is around 27–30 years, with age relaxation for SC, ST, OBC, EWS, Ex-servicemen and other categories as per government rules.
  • Forest Range Officer / ACF and higher competitive posts: Age limit often ranges from 21 to 32 years (varies by recruitment), with relaxations for reserved categories.
  • Exact age limit, cut-off date and relaxation details always depend on the particular notification, so candidates should verify from the official advertisement before applying.

Forest Department Jobs Salary Overview

Forest department salary depends on the post, pay level, state and allowances. Senior officers under Indian Forest Service receive higher pay levels, while Forest Guard, Forester and similar posts fall under state government pay matrices.

Post Pay Level Basic Pay Gross Salary (Approx) Category
Forest Guard / Van Rakshak / Vana Palaka Level 3 ₹21,700 ₹28,000 – ₹35,000 Group C
Forester / Forest Sub-Inspector Level 6 ₹35,400 ₹45,000 – ₹56,000 Group C
Forest Beat Officer / Beat Guard / Junior Ranger Level 4 ₹25,500 ₹33,000 – ₹42,000 Group C
Forest Range Officer (FRO) / Ranger Level 10 ₹56,100 ₹70,000 – ₹90,000 Group B
Assistant Conservator of Forests (ACF) Level 10 ₹56,100 ₹75,000 – ₹95,000 Group B

Principal Chief Conservator (PCCF / HoFF) Level 17 ₹2,25,000 ₹2,25,000 (fixed) Apex Scale

Selection Process & Exam Pattern

The selection process for Forest Department Jobs 2026 typically includes written examinations, physical tests and document verification. Some posts also conduct medical examination and walking tests due to the demanding nature of field duties.

Written Exam, PET/PST & Document Verification

  • Written Exam: Most Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer recruitments start with a written exam based on objective-type questions (MCQs).
  • PET / PST (Physical Efficiency Test / Physical Standard Test): Candidates who qualify written exam are called for running, walking test, height, chest measurement and other physical parameters as per post and gender.
  • Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates must produce original certificates of age, qualification, caste, residence, sports/NCC and other documents mentioned in the notification.
  • Medical Examination: Many forest posts require medical fitness to ensure candidates can perform field duties in forests, wildlife areas and difficult terrain.

Syllabus Highlights for Forest Guard & Ranger

Typical syllabus for written exam may include:

  • General Knowledge & Current Affairs (national, state-level, environment and forest-related topics)
  • General Hindi / English or regional language (grammar, comprehension, vocabulary)
  • General Mathematics / Quantitative Aptitude (up to 10th or 12th level)
  • General Science and Environmental Studies (forest, wildlife, biodiversity, conservation, climate, basic biology)
  • Reasoning Ability (logical and analytical reasoning)

Exact subject-wise marks, number of questions, negative marking and exam duration will be given in the respective forest department notification.

How to Apply for Forest Department Jobs 2026

Most Forest Department Jobs 2026 applications are accepted either online through official websites or offline via prescribed application forms. Candidates should carefully read the official notification before filling the form.

Online Application Process

  1. Visit the official website of the recruiting body (State Forest Department, State PSC, Staff Selection Board or other government portal).
  2. Find the latest link for Forest Guard / Forester / Forest Range Officer / other forest posts and open the detailed advertisement.
  3. Complete the registration process with basic details like name, mobile number, email ID and verify using OTP if required.
  4. Fill the online application form with personal, educational, address and category information exactly as per documents.
  5. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature and other certificates in the prescribed format and size.
  6. Pay the application fee online through Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card or UPI as per your convenience.
  7. Finally, submit the form and take a printout or PDF of the submitted application and payment receipt for future reference.

Documents Required & Application Fee

  • Recent passport size photograph and signature (scanned)
  • Class 10th and 12th mark sheets and certificates
  • Degree / Diploma certificates for graduate-level posts
  • Caste / Category certificate (SC, ST, OBC, EWS, etc.)
  • Domicile / Residence certificate if required by the state
  • NCC, Sports, Ex-serviceman and other quota certificates if applicable
  • Valid ID proof (Aadhaar Card, PAN, Voter ID, Driving Licence, Passport, etc.)

Why Choose Forest Department Jobs?

Forest department jobs are popular among candidates who love nature, wildlife and field work. These jobs offer job security, government benefits and a chance to work in forest conservation, wildlife protection, eco-tourism and community development.

  • Stable government job with regular salary, pension/NPS and allowances
  • Opportunity to work in forests, wildlife sanctuaries, national parks and protected areas
  • Contribution to environment protection, afforestation and biodiversity conservation
  • Good promotion avenues from Forest Guard to Forester, Forest Range Officer and higher posts in many states.

The CSC e-Governance Services India Limited (CSC) has published the Aadhaar Supervisor/ Operator Notification 2026 on 02 April 2026. The recruitment drive aims to fill 344 Aadhaar Supervisor/ Operator positions across multiple states. Candidates within the minimum age of 18 years and above and holding qualifications such as 12th Pass / ITI / Polytechnic Diploma along with UIDAI Aadhaar Operator/Supervisor Certification are eligible to apply online at https://www.csc.gov.in . The application process will be active from 02 April 2026 to 20 May 2026, and candidates can easily access the notification PDF and apply link below.

FOLLOW US

CSC e-Governance Services Indian Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF Download

The CSC e-Governance Services Indian Aadhaar Supervisor/ Operator Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF has been released on 02-04-2026 at csc.gov.in/. Check the complete job details, vacancy, age limit, application fee, selection process and how to apply from the article. You can Check all Latest  Updates of All Central Government Jobs and State Government Jobs.

CSC e-Governance Services Indian Aadhaar Supervisor/ Operator Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Name of the Post: CSC e-Governance Services Indian Aadhaar Supervisor/ Operator Online Form 2026

Post Date: 07-04-2026

Latest Update: 05-05-2026

Total Vacancy: 344 Posts

Brief Information: The CSC e-Governance Services Indian has announced a notification for the recruitment of Aadhaar Supervisor/ Operator Vacancy. Eligible Candidates who are interested in the vacancy details & completed all eligibility criteria can read the notification & Apply.

CSC e-Governance Services Indian Recruitment 2026 Notification Overview

The CSC e-Governance Services Indian has officially released a recruitment notification for Aadhaar Supervisor/ Operator. For all details regarding the recruitment process, eligibility, and application procedure, refer to the official notification. Eligible candidates can download it from the link below.

CSC e-Governance Services Indian

Aadhaar Supervisor/ Operator Vacancy 2026

WWW.OnlyJobs

Application Fee

  • Refer Official Notification

CSC e-Governance Services Indian Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

  • Starting Date for Apply Online: 02-04-2026
  • Last Date for Apply Online: 20-05-2026

CSC e-Governance Services Indian Recruitment 2026 Age Limit

  • Minimum Age Limit: 18 Years or above
  • Age relaxation is applicable as per rules.

Qualification

  • 12th (Intermediate/Senior Secondary) or  Matriculation +2 Years ITI or Matriculation +3 Years Polytechnic Diploma.
  • Candidate Should have Aadhaar Operator/Supervisor certificate issued by Testing & certifying agency authorized by UIDAI for delivering Aadhaar service
  • Should have basic computer skills

Remuneration

  • Minimum wages of the respective state set for semi-skilled manpower

Other Terms and Conditions

  • You can take the Aadhaar Supervisor exam through this link: https://uidai.nseitexams.com/UIDAI/LoginAction_input.action
  • When you apply for the exam, an authorization request is sent to the State Team.
  • Once they approve the request, you will be able to appear for the Aadhaar Supervisor exam.
  • You can take the Aadhaar LMS Certification once your LMS ID and Password are created and approved. After that, you will be able to appear for the LMS Certification through this link:   https://e-learning.uidai.gov.in

How to Apply for CSC e‑Governance Services Indian Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator Recruitment 2026

Candidates who meet the eligibility criteria can apply online only through the official CSC e‑Governance Services Indian website. Follow the steps given below to submit your application form.

Visit Official Website

  • Go to the official CSC e‑Governance Services Indian website: csc.gov.in.

  • On the homepage, navigate to the Recruitment/Careers/Latest Opening section.

Open Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator Application Link

  • Find and click on the notification/link titled “CSC e‑Governance Services Indian Aadhaar Supervisor/ Operator Recruitment 2026” or similar.

  • Click on the Apply Online button to proceed to the application page.

Registration (New Users)

  • Click on New Registration (if applying for the first time).

  • Enter basic details such as Name, Mobile Number, Email ID, Date of Birth, and create a password.

  • Submit the details to generate your Registration ID and Password (note these for future login).

Login and Fill Application Form

  • Login using your Registration ID and Password.

  • Fill in the online application form carefully with personal details, contact details, educational qualification, Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator certificate details, and preferred state/district.

  • Ensure that all details match your certificates and Aadhaar data.

Upload Documents

  • Upload scanned copies of the following documents in the prescribed format and size (as mentioned in the notification):

    • Recent passport size photograph

    • Signature

    • 10th/12th/ITI/Polytechnic Diploma certificates and mark sheets

    • Aadhaar Operator/Supervisor Certificate issued by UIDAI‑authorized Testing & Certifying Agency

    • Any other documents required as per notification

  • Preview the uploaded documents and confirm that they are clear and readable.

Application Fee Payment (If Applicable)

  • Check the Application Fee section in the official notification.

  • If any fee is prescribed, pay it online through available modes such as Debit Card, Credit Card, Net Banking, or UPI.

  • After successful payment, download/print the fee receipt.

Final Submission

  • Carefully review all the details filled in the application form in the Preview section.

  • If required, make corrections before final submission.

  • Click on Final Submit to complete the application process.

Print Application Form

  • After successful submission, download and take a printout of the final submitted application form for future reference.

  • Keep the Registration ID, Password, and printout safe for further recruitment stages.

BPCL Entry Level Profiles Eligibility Criteria 2026

Candidates must fulfil the essential educational qualification and minimum work-experience as on 01.04.2026.

Junior Executive (Engineering):

  • Educational Qualification: Diploma in Engineering (3-year course) in Mechanical / Electrical / Instrumentation / Electronics / Civil / Chemical Engineering from a recognized University with minimum 55% aggregate (relaxed to 50% for SC/ST/PwBD).
  • Work Experience: Minimum 5 years of continuous post-qualification relevant work experience (including minimum 1 year in managerial/supervisory role) in sectors like POL Terminal/Depot, LPG Bottling Plant, Pipeline, Refinery, Petrochemical, etc.

Junior Executive (Accounts)

  • Educational Qualification: Graduate in any discipline with minimum 55% + Inter CA / Inter CMA.
  • Work Experience: Minimum 5 years of post-qualification relevant experience in finance function.

Junior Executive (Wayside Amenities)

  • Educational Qualification: B.Com / BBA / B.Sc / BMS / BHM with minimum 55% (relaxed to 50% for SC/ST/PwBD).
  • Work Experience:  Minimum 5 years of relevant post-qualification experience (at least 3 years in Facility Management / QSR / Retail / Hospitality, etc.).

Secretary

  • Educational Qualification: Class X + Class XII + Bachelor's Degree (3-year course) with minimum 55% in each (relaxed to 50% for SC/ST/PwBD).
  • Work Experience: Minimum 5 years as Secretarial Assistant / Secretary or similar roles.

Associate Executive (Quality Assurance)

  • Educational Qualification: M.Sc (Chemistry) with specialization in specified branches with minimum 60% (relaxed to 55% for SC/ST/PwBD).
  • Work Experience: Minimum 3 years post-qualification relevant work experience in laboratory in Petroleum / Oil & Gas / Petro-Chemical / Chemical industry.

Associate Executive (Official Language Implementation)

 

  • Educational Qualification: Master’s in Hindi (2-year course) with minimum 55% (relaxed to 50% for SC/ST/PwBD) + English studied in Class XII / Graduation with minimum 55%.
  • Work Experience: Minimum 3 years of post-qualification work experience in translation (English ↔ Hindi).

Common Note for All Profiles

  • Only Indian Nationals are eligible.

BPCL Entry Level Profiles Application Fee 2026

Age relaxations as per Presidential Directives for OBC-NCL / SC / ST / PwBD / Ex-Servicemen (cumulative maximum 10 years).

Profile Upper Age Limit (as on 01.04.2026)

Junior Executive (Wayside Amenities) 32 years
Secretary BPCL 32 years

Associate Executive (Official Language Implementation) 35 years

BPCL Entry Level Profiles Application Fee 2026

Category Application Fee
UR / OBC-NCL / EWS ₹1180/- (₹1000 + 18% GST + payment gateway charges if applicable)
SC / ST / PwBD Exempted (No Application Fee)

BPCL Entry Level Profiles Selection Process 2026

  • Multi-stage selection process may comprise Application Screening, Written / Computer Based Test, Case Based Discussion, Group Task, Personal Interview etc.
  • Provisional selection based on Relevant work-experience, Significant on-the-job achievements, Performance in various selection processes.
  • Final appointment subject to successful completion of Pre-Employment Medical Examination.

How to Apply for BPCL Recruitment 2026

Candidates must apply ONLINE only through the official BPCL website. No other mode of application will be accepted. The online application process is open from 18 April 2026 to 24 May 2026 (23:59 hrs).

Step-by-Step Application Process

  1. Check Eligibility: Ensure that you meet all the eligibility criteria including educational qualification, age limit, and work experience before applying. No eligibility-related queries will be entertained.
  2. Keep Documents Ready: Prepare scanned copies (PDF format) of the following documents:
    • Date of Birth Proof (Class 10th/12th Certificate)
    • Educational Qualification Certificates (Marksheet + Degree/Diploma)
    • Work Experience Certificates (with job role, duration, salary, etc.)
    • Caste/Category Certificate (if applicable)
    • Recent Passport Size Photograph (latest, not older than April 2026)
    • Scanned Signature (not in CAPITAL letters)
    • Last 3 Salary Slips (combined in one PDF)
  3. Register Online:
    • Visit the BPCL Careers page and click on “Apply Online”
    • Click on “New Registration”
    • Enter Name, Email ID, and Mobile Number
    • Note the Registration Number and Password generated
  4. Fill Application Form:
    • Login using credentials
    • Fill personal, educational, and experience details carefully
    • Use “Save & Next” to verify details
  5. Upload Documents: Upload photograph, signature, and required documents as per prescribed format.
  6. Preview Application: Carefully review all details using the Preview Tab before final submission. No changes will be allowed after submission.
  7. Pay Application Fee:
    • Pay via Debit Card / Credit Card / UPI / Net Banking
    • Ensure payment status shows “Completed”
    • Take a printout of payment receipt
  8. Final Submission: Click on “Submit” to complete the application process.

Important Instructions

  • Apply only once; multiple applications may lead to rejection.
  • No hard copy of application needs to be sent to BPCL.
  • Ensure all details match original documents; mismatch may lead to disqualification.
  • Keep your registered email ID and mobile number active for future communication.

BPCL Entry Level Profiles Online Application Form

Eligible candidates who wish to apply for the BPCL Entry Level Profiles can do so by submitting their application form online through the official BPCL website. The application process for the BPCL Recruitment 2026 is completely online, and candidates must ensure that they complete all steps before the deadline.

The online application process for the BPCL Entry Level Profiles Recruitment 2026 is available through the BPCL portal. Eligible candidates are encouraged to fill out the application form and submit the required documents before the last date.

Click Here to Apply Online for BPCL Entry Level Profiles Recruitment 2026

BPCL Entry Level Profiles Remuneration 2026

Designation Pay Scale* Approx. CTC at the minimum of Pay Scale
Junior Executive & Secretary Post 30,000-1,20,000 ₹12.28 Lakhs per annum
Associate Executive 40,000-1,40,000 ₹17.11 Lakhs per annum

Candidates will be inducted at the minimum of the pay scale. In addition to Basic Pay, Dearness Allowance (IDA pattern), HRA (if applicable), Perks & Allowances, Retirement Benefits and Performance Related Pay will be payable as per Corporation’s policy.

Important Instructions for Candidates

  • Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply.
  • The cutoff date for age, qualification, and experience is 01 April 2026.
  • All qualifications must be from AICTE/UGC recognized institutions.
  • Marks should be calculated on total aggregate; no rounding off allowed.
  • CGPA must be converted into percentage as per university norms.
  • Only post-qualification experience will be considered.
  • Internship/training during studies will not be counted.
  • Candidates must fill the application form carefully; details once submitted are final.
  • No changes will be allowed after final submission.
  • Submit only one application; latest application will be considered if multiple are submitted.
  • Candidates working in Govt/PSU must produce NOC during selection.
  • Shortlisting is provisional and subject to document verification.
  • Ineligible candidates at any stage will be disqualified.
  • All communication will be done via registered email only.
  • Selection process participation does not guarantee final selection.
  • Updates/corrigendum will be available only on the official website.
  • Travel Allowance (TA) will be provided as per rules for eligible candidates.
  • BPCL reserves the right to cancel/modify recruitment or change vacancies.
  • Providing false information will lead to disqualification.
  • Canvassing or external influence will result in rejection.
  • Apply only through the official portal; beware of fraudulent agents.
  • Any dispute will be subject to Mumbai jurisdiction.

Candidates are required to upload their photograph (390 x 520 pixels, max 120 KB) and signature (240 x 240 pixels, max 60 KB - signature in Capital letters will NOT be accepted). The following enclosures must be uploaded along with the e-application (max 250 KB each).

Apply Online at: www.ib.tmbonline.bank.in/tmb_careers/

  • Note your Unique Registration Number and Password - failing which you will not be able to proceed further.
  • Signature in Capital letters will NOT be accepted.
  • No vigilance case or adverse observation from RBI/Statutory Authority shall be pending against any candidate identified for appointment.
  • For Law Officer post - only candidates from Maharashtra (Mumbai) state are eligible.
  • IT vacancies will be posted at Chennai; GNC Head at Ernakulam; VP Accounts & DVP Secretarial Section at Thoothukudi; Law Officer at Maharashtra; remaining posts are PAN India.
  • Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification.

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 Overview

Field Details

Advertisement No. RBISB/DA/01/2026-27
Post Name Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) (On Probation) – General / DEPR / DSIM Cadres

Phase-II Exam – DEPR / DSIM Cadre 26-07-2026

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 60 vacancies have been announced across three cadres – General, DEPR, and DSIM. Vacancies are distributed across GEN/UR, EWS, OBC, SC, and ST categories.

Post Total
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – General Cadre 40
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – DEPR Cadre 10
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – DSIM Cadre 10
Total 60

Sr. No. Post PwBD Category A PwBD Category B PwBD Category C PwBD Category D
1 Grade 'B' (DR) – General – – 1 1

3 Grade 'B' (DR) – DSIM 1 – – –

Category A: Blindness & Low Vision; B: Deaf & Hard of Hearing; C: Locomotor disability; D: Autism, intellectual disability, specific learning disability and mental illness

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on April 01, 2026. Nationality conditions apply – the post is open to citizens of India and certain other categories as specified in the notification, subject to eligibility certificate from the Government of India where required.

The educational qualification must be from a University incorporated by an Act of the Central or State Legislature, or an institution declared Deemed University under UGC Act, 1956, or an equivalent foreign university recognized by the Association of Indian Universities.

Educational Qualification

Post Minimum Educational Qualifications (as on April 01, 2026)
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – General Graduation in any discipline with minimum 60% marks (50% for SC/ST/PwBD if posts reserved for them) OR Post-Graduation with minimum 55% marks (pass marks for SC/ST/PwBD if posts reserved for them) in aggregate of all semesters/years.
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – DEPR Essential: MA/MSc in Economics or related Economics/Finance disciplines with minimum 55% marks (50% for SC/ST/PwBD if posts reserved) from a recognized Indian or Foreign University. Desirable: Doctorate in Economics, or research/teaching experience, or publication in standard journals.
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – DSIM Essential: Master's Degree with minimum 55% marks in Statistics/Mathematics/Data Science/AI/ML or related fields OR Four-year Bachelor's Degree with minimum 60% marks in the above fields (50% for SC/ST/PwBD if posts reserved). Desirable: Doctorate in related subjects; research/teaching experience and publications.

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must have attained the age of 21 years and must not have attained the age of 30 years as on April 01, 2026 (born not earlier than April 02, 1996 and not later than April 01, 2005). The upper age limit is relaxed for various categories as detailed below.

General/EWS candidates who have already appeared six times for Phase-I Examination for this post in the past are not eligible to apply. No such restriction applies to SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates if posts are reserved for them.

Category Age Limit
General (UR) / EWS 21 to 30 years (as on 01-04-2026)
SC / ST 5 years relaxation (max 35 years)
OBC 3 years relaxation (max 33 years)
PwBD – Gen/EWS 10 years relaxation (max 40 years)
PwBD – OBC 13 years relaxation (max 43 years)
PwBD – SC/ST 15 years relaxation (max 45 years)
Ex-Servicemen / Ex-Bank Employees 5 years relaxation
Candidates with M.Phil. Upper age limit 32 years
Candidates with Ph.D. Upper age limit 34 years
Bank Officers (experience-based relaxation) Up to 3 years relaxation; upper age not to exceed 33 years including experience
DEPR/DSIM – Master's with Research/Teaching Experience Up to 3 years relaxation

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates applying for the post of Officers in Grade 'B' are required to pay a non-refundable fee/intimation charges as specified below. The fee must be paid online through the RBI application portal. Fee paid will not be refunded under any circumstances nor held in reserve for any other examination or selection.

Candidates submitting applications without the prescribed fee/intimation charges will be summarily rejected. Candidates are therefore advised to confirm their eligibility and verify all details before making payment.

Category Charges Amount
SC / ST / PwBD Intimation Charges only ₹100/- + 18% GST
GEN / OBC / EWS Application Fee including Intimation Charges ₹850/- + 18% GST
RBI Staff (eligible Staff Candidates) Nil Nil

Bank/Transaction charges are to be borne by the candidate.

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for RBI Grade B Recruitment 2026 consists of Phase-I Online Examination, Phase-II Online/Written Examination, and Interview. Candidates who qualify Phase-I will be shortlisted for Phase-II based on aggregate marks. Only those who clear Phase-II will be called for the Interview.

No candidate will be called for the Interview unless he/she appears in Phase-II examination. The final merit list will be prepared by adding marks secured in Phase-II and Interview (and grace marks as applicable). If a candidate remains absent in the Interview, his/her candidature will not be considered for final selection.

The selection list recommended by the Board remains valid for the period as decided by the Board/Bank.

Stage 1 – Phase-I Online Examination (Grade 'B' DR – General)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Single Paper General Awareness, English Language, Quantitative Aptitude, Reasoning Objective MCQ 200 120 Minutes

Stage 1 – Phase-I Online/Written Examination (DEPR Cadre)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Paper-I Economics – Objective Type Online MCQ 100 120 Minutes
Paper-II English – Descriptive Typed on keyboard 100 120 Minutes

Stage 1 – Phase-I Online Examination (DSIM Cadre)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Paper-I Statistics – Objective Type Online MCQ 100 120 Minutes

Stage 2 – Phase-II Online Examination (Grade 'B' DR – General)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Paper-I Economic and Social Issues 50% Objective + 50% Descriptive 100 120 Minutes
Paper-II English (Writing Skills) Descriptive (typed) 100 90 Minutes
Paper-III General Finance and Management 50% Objective + 50% Descriptive 100 120 Minutes
Grand Total     300  

Stage 2 – Phase-II Written Examination (DEPR Cadre)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Paper-I Economics – Descriptive Pen/Paper (questions on screen) 100 120 Minutes
Paper-II Economics – Descriptive Pen/Paper (questions on screen) 100 120 Minutes
Total     400 (incl. Phase-I)  

Stage 2 – Phase-II Written Examination (DSIM Cadre)

Paper Subject Type Marks Duration
Paper-II Statistics – Descriptive Pen/Paper (questions on screen) 100 180 Minutes
Paper-III English – Descriptive Typed on keyboard 100 90 Minutes
Total     300 (incl. Phase-I)  

Stage 3 – Interview: 75 Marks (All Cadres)

Candidates will also undergo a Personality Assessment before the Interview. No marks are allotted for Personality Assessment and it does not form part of the final selection criteria. Candidates may opt for Interview in Hindi or English.

Qualifying Marks

Category Minimum Qualifying Marks
All Categories Minimum marks separately for each test/paper as prescribed by the Board; minimum aggregate cut-off for Phase-II shortlisting decided by the Board in relation to number of vacancies

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Exam Syllabus

Phase-I – General Cadre (200 Marks, Objective MCQ, 120 Minutes)

The paper covers General Awareness, English Language, Quantitative Aptitude, and Reasoning. Separate time will be allotted for each section. Detailed information will be provided in the Information Handout available with the Admit Card.

Phase-II – General Cadre

Paper-I (Economic and Social Issues): Growth and Development (National Income, poverty alleviation, sustainable development); Indian Economy (economic history, monetary and fiscal policy since 1991, Indian money and financial markets, public finance, agriculture, services sector); Globalization (BoP, export-import policy, IMF, World Bank, WTO); Social Structure in India (multiculturalism, demographic trends, urbanization, gender issues, social justice).

Paper-II (English – Writing Skills): Writing skills including expression and understanding of the topic.

Paper-III (General Finance and Management): Financial system (structure of financial institutions, RBI functions, banking system, digital payments); Financial markets (primary and secondary markets); General topics (financial risk management, derivatives, financial inclusion, corporate governance, Union Budget, accounting basics, inflation); Management (fundamentals, organizational behaviour, motivation, leadership, ethics, corporate governance, communication).

Phase-I – DEPR Cadre (Paper-I, Objective – Economics)

Microeconomics, Macroeconomics (IS-LM Model, schools of economic thought), International Economics, Theories of Economic Growth and Development, Public Finance, Environmental Economics, Quantitative Methods in Economics, Current developments in Indian Economy.

Phase-II – DEPR Cadre (Descriptive – Economics)

Paper-I covers Microeconomics Module (consumer theory, production theory, market theory, welfare economics) and Macroeconomics Module (national income accounting, employment and output, inflation, money and banking, growth theories, international trade, public finance) with equal weightage. Paper-II covers Quantitative Methods in Economics (mathematical methods, statistical methods, econometrics) and Indian Economy – Policy and Trends (fiscal policy, monetary policy, banking sector, inflation, external sector, sectoral developments) with equal weightage.

Phase-I – DSIM Cadre (Paper-I, Objective – Statistics) and Phase-II (Paper-II, Descriptive – Statistics)

Topics covered across both papers: Theory of Probability, Probability Distributions and Sampling Theory; Linear Models and Economic Statistics; Statistical Inference (Estimation, Testing of Hypothesis, Non-Parametric Tests); Stochastic Processes; Multivariate Analysis; Econometrics and Time Series; Optimization and Statistical Computing; Data Science, Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Techniques; Database and Data Warehouse Management.

How to Apply for RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the Bank's website www.rbi.org.in. No other means/mode of application will be accepted. The online portal will be active from 29-04-2026 to 20-05-2026 (6:00 PM). Candidates desirous of applying for both Grade 'B' (DR)-General and Grade 'B' (DR)-DEPR or DSIM must apply using separate links and pay fees for both. Digi Locker has been integrated with the online application form for voluntary submission of credentials.

Candidates should avoid submitting multiple applications; if done unavoidably, only the application with the higher Registration ID (RID) will be considered valid, and fee paid against other RIDs will not be adjusted. After the closing date, a 2-day edit window will be available for making corrections in limited fields. Candidates are not required to upload supporting documents at the time of online application; documents will be verified at the Interview stage only.

Documents Required at Interview Stage:

  • Age proof (birth certificate or Class X marksheet/certificate)
  • Educational qualification certificates and marksheets
  • SC / ST / OBC / PwBD / EWS / Ex-SM certificate (if applicable) in prescribed format
  • Photo identity proof (PAN Card / Passport / Driving Licence / Voter's Card / Aadhaar, etc.)
  • Online application form printout with fee payment receipt

Candidates working in Government/PSU/Public Enterprises must inform their employer in writing before applying and submit an undertaking to this effect at the time of online application. At the time of joining, proper discharge certificates from their employer must be produced.

Apply Online at: https://www.rbi.org.in

Important Instructions for RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Only eligible citizens (as defined in the notification) are eligible to apply; nationality certificate required for certain categories.
  • Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on April 01, 2026.
  • No hard copy applications will be accepted – online submission only.
  • Recruitment fee/intimation charges are non-refundable; applications without prescribed fee will be summarily rejected.
  • Government/PSU employees must submit an undertaking about informing their employer and bring discharge certificate at the time of joining.
  • Mobile phones, pagers, and all electronic devices are banned inside examination/interview premises; physical calculators are not permitted in any phase.
  • Candidates reporting after Gate Closure Time specified on the Admit Card will not be permitted to take the examination.
  • Candidates are advised to visit https://www.rbi.org.in regularly for updates; all corrigenda will be published on the Bank's website only.
  • Phase-I paper (General Cadre) will be set in English; all other papers (except English papers) will be set bilingually in Hindi and English.
  • Negative marking on MCQ questions will apply as prescribed; detailed marking scheme will be in the Information Handout.
  • For any problem in filling the form or downloading Admit Card, queries may be raised through http://cgrs.ibps.in mentioning the post and cadre in the subject line.
  • All decisions of the Board regarding eligibility, conduct of examinations, selection, and other matters are final and binding; any resultant dispute is subject to sole jurisdiction of Courts at Mumbai.

UP Cooperative Bank (UPCISB) Age Limit 2026

The age limit to apply for UPCISB Clerk, Junior Manager and More Posts Recruitment 2026 is 21 to 40 years. Age relaxation will be provided to reserved category candidates as per government rules.

UP Cooperative Bank (UPCISB) Application Fee 2026

  • Application fee is to be paid only through online mode via debit card, credit card, internet banking, UPI, IMPS, cash card or mobile wallet during 25 April 2026 to 15 May 2026.
  • Bank transaction charges for payment of application fee will be borne by the candidate and the fee once paid is non-refundable.
Category Application Fee
Unreserved (General) / OBC / EWS Rs. 500/-
Scheduled Caste (SC) / Scheduled Tribe (ST) Rs. 250/-

UP Cooperative Bank (UPCISB) Selection Process 2026

  • The selection process consists of an online Preliminary Examination (if required) and a Main Examination conducted by UPCISB.
  • Based on the Preliminary Exam, up to 15 times the number of vacancies will be shortlisted for the Main Exam.
  • On the basis of Main Exam result, up to 3 times the number of posts will be called for interview (where applicable) and up to 5 times the number of posts for proficiency test, wherever prescribed.
  • Both Preliminary and Main examinations will be objective-type online tests with multiple choice questions and negative marking of 1/4 mark for each wrong answer.

How to Apply for UP Cooperative Bank (UPCISB) Recruitment 2026

  • Candidates must apply online only through the official website of U.P. Co-operative Institutional Service Board: https://www.upcisb.upsdc.gov.in.
  • First, complete “New Registration” by entering name, contact details and email ID to generate a registration number and password.
  • Fill in the online application form carefully using the “Save and Next” option, upload scanned photograph, signature, left-hand thumb impression and handwritten declaration as per the prescribed specifications.
  • Pay the application fee through the integrated payment gateway, ensure e-Receipt generation, and take a printout of the final submitted application form and fee receipt for future reference.

UP Cooperative Bank (UPCISB) Salary 2026

  • The notification provides detailed pay scales for each post, such as specific minimum and maximum pay in the respective pay bands for Manager, Junior Manager, Assistant/Cashier, Assistant Manager, Accountant, Junior Engineer, Assistant Engineer and other posts.

  • Pay scales differ institution-wise and post-wise (for example, different pay bands for Junior Manager in different District Co-operative Banks), and all posts are permanent with regular pay as per the respective co-operative institution’s rules.

Important Instructions for Candidates

  • Candidates must thoroughly read and understand all instructions in the advertisement before filling the online application form.
  • The photograph, signature, thumb impression and handwritten declaration must be clear and as per the prescribed size and format, otherwise the application may be rejected.
  • No change is permitted in key details after clicking on “Complete Registration”, so all information (name, father’s name, etc.) must match official certificates.
  • Candidates should keep their registered email ID and mobile number active till completion of the recruitment process for receiving admit card and other communication from UPCISB.
  • Minimum efficiency standard (qualifying marks) and negative marking rules in the exam must be noted carefully, as incorrect answers will attract penalty.

A total of 30 vacancies have been announced across 4 sports disciplines - Cricket, Kabaddi, Table Tennis, and Football - spread across posting locations in Delhi, Mumbai, and Chennai. Out of 30 posts, 24 are for Male candidates and 6 for Female candidates.

Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026 Eligibility Criteria

School Team in National Sports/Games for Schools conducted by All India School Games Federation.

*Relaxation in upper age limit up to a maximum of five years to sportspersons considered meritorious, as per DOPT, Govt of India O.M. No. DOPT-1720781414772 dated 04.03.2020. Ten years relaxation in the case of SC/ST sportspersons.

Authority for Awarding Sports Certificate

S.N. Competition Authority for Awarding Certificate
1 International Competition Secretary of National Federation of the Game concerned.
2 National Competition Secretary of the National Federation or Secretary of the State / U.T. Association of the Game concerned.
3 Inter-University Tournaments Dean of Sports or other officer in overall charge of Sports of the University concerned.
4 National / Sports / Games for Schools Director or Additional / Joint or Deputy Director in overall charge of sports / games for Schools in the Directorate of Public Instructions / Education of the State.
5 Khelo India University Games / Khelo India Youth Games / Khelo India Winter Games / Khelo India Para Games Officer designated / Officer in charge from Sports Authority of India (SAI).

Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026 Age Limit

The minimum age to apply is 20 years and the maximum age is 28 years for General/EWS category candidates, calculated as on the cut-off date of 01.04.2026. 

S No Category Age Relaxation Relaxation to Meritorious Sportspersons as per DOPT guidelines* Total Age Relaxation*
1 General / EWS Nil 5 Years 5 Years
2 Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe 5 years 5 years 10 years
3 Other Backward Classes (NCL) 3 years 5 years 8 years

*This concession will be available only to those meritorious sportspersons who satisfy all other eligibility criteria and furnish a certificate in the form and from an authority prescribed as per the guidelines issued by the Government from time to time.

Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026 Application Fee

The application fee is non-refundable and must be paid online at the time of registration regardless of whether the candidate is shortlisted for Field Trials or not. General, EWS, and OBC candidates are required to pay ₹850/- (inclusive of GST) plus applicable payment gateway charges. SC, ST, ESM/DESM (Ex-Servicemen/Disabled Ex-Servicemen), and Women candidates are required to pay ₹175/- (intimation charges only, inclusive of GST) plus applicable payment gateway charges.

Payment can be made through Debit Card, Credit Card, Internet Banking, UPI, or any other available online payment mode. Transaction charges, if any, are to be borne by the candidate. Once fee payment is completed successfully, an e-receipt and application form will be generated, which should be printed and retained. The Bank will not entertain any refund requests under any circumstances.

Category Application Fee
General / EWS / OBC ₹850/- (Inclusive of GST) + Payment Gateway Charges
SC / ST / ESM / DESM / Women ₹175/- (Inclusive of GST) + Payment Gateway Charges

Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026 Pay Scale & Benefits

Selected candidates will be placed in the clerical cadre pay scale as applicable to Customer Service Associates in Bank of Baroda. The starting basic pay is ₹24,050/- per month and the scale progresses through multiple incremental stages over a period of 20 years, reaching up to ₹64,480/-. Increments are applied at different rates at various stages of the pay scale as detailed below. 

Pay Scale (as amended from time to time)
24050 – 1340/3 – 28070 – 1650/3 – 33020 – 2000/4 – 41020 – 2340/7 – 57400 – 4400/1 – 61800 – 2680/1 – 64480 (20 years)

Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026 Selection Process

The selection process consists of two main stages: Shortlisting/Screening of applications followed by Field Trials for shortlisted candidates. In the first stage, applications will be screened against the eligibility criteria including educational qualifications, relevant sports experience, domain expertise, and suitability. Meeting the minimum eligibility criteria does not automatically entitle a candidate to be called for Field Trials - the Bank may call candidates in a ratio as deemed appropriate at its sole discretion. The shortlisting criteria are internal and confidential and will not be shared with candidates.

Shortlisted candidates will be called for Field Trials at a designated venue, the evaluation criteria and qualifying cut-off for which will be determined solely by the Bank. No requests for change of centre, venue, date, or time for Field Trials will be entertained. The Bank's decision on shortlisting and selection at all stages is final and binding. Selected candidates must be Active Sportspersons at the time of joining and fit enough to participate in future tournaments. All selected sportspersons will be required to sign a written undertaking to play for the Bank's team whenever required.

Stage Details
Stage 1: Shortlisting / Screening Applications screened based on eligibility criteria, sports experience, qualifications, and suitability as determined by the Bank. Criteria are internal and confidential.
Stage 2: Field Trials Shortlisted candidates called for Field Trials at designated venue. Evaluation criteria and qualifying cut-off determined solely by the Bank.
Stage 3: Document Verification Original documents to be produced at the time of Field Trials / as called by the Bank. Failure to produce documents will debar candidature.

Obligations of Candidates Recruited as Sportspersons

Obligation Details
Playing for the Bank Sportsperson must play for the Bank / Bank's team whenever and wherever necessary.
Permission for External Events Prior permission required to participate in State / National / International events for other organisations. Bank reserves the right to withhold permission.
Misconduct Refusal to play for the Bank or playing elsewhere without permission will be treated as misconduct and attract disciplinary proceedings.
Written Undertaking All recruited sportspersons must give a written undertaking at the time of appointment.

How to Apply for Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026

The application process is entirely online - no other mode of application will be accepted. Candidates must visit the Bank's official website www.bankofbaroda.bank.in and apply through the Careers → Current Opportunities section. Before starting the application, candidates must ensure they have a valid personal email ID and active mobile number, a scanned passport-size photograph (20–50 KB, JPG format), a scanned signature in black ink on white paper (10–20 KB, JPG format), and scanned PDF copies of all required documents within the specified file sizes (maximum 500 KB per file).

Candidates are advised to fill the application with utmost care as no changes will be permitted after clicking the SUBMIT button. All details including name, caste, category, date of birth, mobile number, and email ID will be treated as final. Candidates must not submit more than one application; in case of multiple submissions, only the last valid completed application will be retained and fees for earlier applications will be forfeited. A printout of the system-generated application form and e-receipt should be retained for future reference.

  • Visit: www.bankofbaroda.bank.in → Careers → Current Opportunities
  • Register online and fill the application form carefully in all fields.
  • Upload scanned photograph, signature, and all required documents as per specifications in Annexure I.
  • Pay the application fee online via Debit Card / Credit Card / Internet Banking / UPI.
  • On successful payment, download and retain the e-receipt and application form printout.
  • Produce original documents at the time of Field Trials as per the document list in Section 10.4 of the notification.

Documents Required for Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026

S.No. Document
i Printout of the valid Call Letter
ii Valid system-generated printout of the online application form
iii Proof of Date of Birth (Birth Certificate / SSLC / Std. X Marksheet or Certificate with DOB)
iv Photo Identity Proof (PAN Card / Passport / Driving Licence / Voter's Card / Bank Passbook with photo / Aadhar Card / Employee ID / Gazetted Officer ID)
v Certificate(s) / Awards / Medals fulfilling Sports Qualification Eligibility Criteria
vi Individual Semester/Year-wise Mark Sheets & Certificates for educational qualifications including final degree/diploma certificate
vii Caste Certificate in prescribed format (for SC / ST / OBC category candidates) - as per Annexure II
viii OBC certificate with Non-Creamy Layer clause (valid within one year as on date of advertisement)
ix EWS Income & Asset Certificate (for EWS candidates) - as per prescribed format
x Service / Discharge Book with pension payment order and rank proof (for Ex-Servicemen)
xi No Objection Certificate from employer (for candidates serving in Govt / PSU / Nationalised Bank)
xii Relevant documents in support of work experience (appointment letter, salary slip, relieving letter, etc.) wherever applicable
xiii Disability Certificate (Form I / II / III as applicable) for PWD candidates

Important Instructions for Candidates

Candidates must read the full official notification carefully before filling the application. All eligibility criteria are to be met as on the cut-off date of 01.04.2026. Change of category is not permitted at any stage after submission of the online application. Candidates should not furnish false, fabricated, or tampered information at any stage - doing so will result in disqualification or termination even after joining. Any canvassing or creating undue influence will lead to disqualification. All legal proceedings arising from this recruitment can only be instituted at courts in Mumbai. Signatures must remain consistent across all documents and correspondence throughout the recruitment process. Selected candidates will be governed by the Bank's service conditions including provisions related to probation (6 months), confirmation, disciplinary rules, and execution of service bonds. Candidates with adverse credit history, criminal cases, or adverse reports from CIBIL or other credit agencies will be ineligible. The Bank reserves the right to reject, modify, increase/decrease vacancies, or cancel the recruitment process at any stage without assigning any reason.

  • Online application once submitted cannot be modified. Fill with utmost care.
  • Keep email ID and mobile number active till completion of the recruitment process.
  • No request for change of Field Trial centre, venue, date or time will be entertained.
  • Maintain a minimum CIBIL score of 680 at the time of joining.
  • Probation period is 6 months from the date of joining.
  • All announcements / corrigendum will be posted only on the Bank's official website.
  • No separate communication will be sent to candidates who are not shortlisted or not selected.

How to Calculate Your Score Using SBI Clerk Mains Answer Key 2026 ?

Use this formula to estimate your score:

Total Score = (Correct Answers × Marks per Question) – (Wrong Answers × Negative Marking)

Marking Scheme for SBI Clerk Mains Exam:

  • Correct Answer: +1 mark (most sections), +2 marks for Computer Aptitude
  • Wrong Answer: -1/4 mark (negative marking)
  • Unattempted: 0 marks

The Central Bank of India has released the official Counselor Recruitment 2026 notification on 06 May 2026 at https://www.centralbankofindia.co.in. A total of 3 vacancies have been announced under this recruitment for FLC Anuppur, FLC Dindori, and FLC Shahdol. Eligible candidates aged between 45 and 65 years who are graduates in any discipline and computer literate with knowledge of MS Office, internet, and local language typing can apply through offline mode. The last date to submit the application form is 22 May 2026. Candidates must send the duly filled application form along with self-attested copies of required documents via Speed Post/Registered Post to the address mentioned in the notification. The official notification PDF and offline application link are provided below for reference.
